channel.title: Code Map :: Choria Configuration Manager
channel.description: A guided deep-dive into the CCM codebase.

item.title: Architecture
item.description: Package layering and the three seams that keep the engine composable: the model contracts, the provider registry, and the manager.

item.title: The Resource-Provider Model
item.description: How a concrete resource type is built, and how one resource is driven to its desired state: decide, act, and verify.

item.title: The Apply Engine
item.description: How a manifest of many resources is parsed, resolved against data, and executed in order, with require as a gate and events as the shared state.

item.title: The Agent
item.description: The background loop that applies manifests on a timer, watches remote sources, refreshes facts and data, and remediates failing health checks.

item.title: Data, Facts, and Templates
item.description: How facts are gathered, how Hiera layers a hierarchy into resolved data, and how templates render that data into resource fields.

item.title: Registration and Discovery
item.description: How stable resources announce themselves into a JetStream store keyed by subject, and how other nodes look them up or watch them live.

item.title: Observability
item.description: How each apply becomes a recorded event, a Prometheus counter, and a session summary, and how health checks feed remediation.

item.title: Reference and Map
item.description: The CLI command surface, a source map of every package, the key types and where they are explained, and a glossary.
